summaries.131510: Comments discuss whether Robolectric or adb-based coordinate tapping could simulate real IME behavior, with the adb approach flagged as flake-prone. A related Super Editor issue is linked showing real-world impact. No concrete proposal or PR emerged; the issue remains open as a feature request with no planned implementation.
summaries.140662: PR #146995 (adding settable focusNode and textEditingController to RawAutocomplete) is proposed as a partial solution, with a DartPad proof-of-concept showing a chevron-triggered dropdown. The original reporter rejects the approach as relying on non-public internal behavior; the thread is stalled, with the feature tagged for @victorsanni's related work on controlling when the options view shows.
summaries.93373: Comments confirm the root cause: TextInputType.streetAddress falls back to TextInputType.text on web/Linux, which does not allow newlines (only TextInputType.multiline does). The issue is confirmed on macOS desktop and Linux in addition to web. A related PR #90211 was merged but did not fix this; the issue remains open with no dedicated fix.
summaries.150260: Confirmed reproducible on macOS with VoiceOver: when the field is unfocused 'content is empty' is read, and even after focusing, using VoiceOver's interact command still reads it as empty. Confirmed on stable (3.22.2) and master. CC'd to @chunhtai for accessibility; no fix or PR yet, status is stalled.
summaries.41630: The issue was initially marked as web-specific but confirmed on macOS desktop too (iOS and Android work fine). A commenter identified font-sync as a root cause on web (Safari uses a different default font than Roboto, causing layout offset between the Flutter canvas field and the hidden textarea), and showed that manually adding Roboto CSS to index.html aligns them. A basic scroll-sync via framework-to-engine messaging was also prototyped. The issue remains open with no merged fix.
summaries.23749: The original scenario (focus not re-requestable after Android back button dismisses keyboard) was addressed by the merged onTap callback PR #24536. Comments propose a workaround of calling FocusScope.of(context).requestFocus(FocusNode()) before re-requesting focus to force defocus first, and a hack via TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilderDelegate to call requestKeyboard() directly. Issue scope was narrowed to expose a public API to open the keyboard; still open with no dedicated API.
summaries.119642: The single comment identifies that wrapping TextFormField widgets in an explicit AutofillGroup produces the correct single-form behavior, and asks whether Flutter should default to that. No fix or PR; status is stalled.
summaries.24001: Comments confirm the root cause is that WearOS uses a different keyboard mechanism from standard Android, so any non-WearOS-aware app has the same issue. Reproduced on latest stable (3.22) with the submit/done button not visible. No fix is planned; labeled as device-specific.
summaries.160725: Confirmed reproducible on physical iOS devices (iPhone 13, 15, SE2, 16 Pro) in debug, profile, and release modes on stable; on master the release-mode lag appears improved but debug/profile remain laggy. A commenter identifies a possible root cause: Apple's 2023 WWDC keyboard architecture rework warns that a busy main thread during keyboard open causes delays, and suggests minimizing Scaffold rebuilds during keyboard open. Issue remains open and under investigation.
summaries.172206: Comments show the aria-label issue depends on the excludeSemantics setting: with excludeSemantics true the label is correct after upgrading to 3.32.6, but with excludeSemantics false two separate input elements are rendered (one with the custom label but not interactive, one interactive with only the hint text). Reproduced on stable 3.32.6 and master 3.33.0. @yjbanov notes customer:chalk ran into a similar issue. Status: open, no PR.
summaries.180059: A commenter confirms the crash reproduces in a plain UIKit app with two UITextFields using the same repro sequence, demonstrating this is an underlying iOS bug (UIViewControllerHierarchyInconsistency in TUIEmojiSearchInputViewController), not a Flutter-specific issue. Confirmed specific to Japanese keyboard (not English), reproducible on iPhone 13 and 15. Status: open, tentatively P2.
summaries.137781: Comments clarify that only the paste action (not cut or copy) bypasses enableInteractiveSelection via three-finger gestures on iOS, since text is not selectable anyway. Also noted that three-finger hold shows the toolbar. Issue re-labeled from design to iOS team. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.162582: Root cause identified in comments: changing obscureText triggers TextInput.updateConfig on the platform channel, and the iOS embedder resets something during that call causing keyboard hints to flicker. Confirmed on iPhone 15 Pro (iOS 18.2) with stable 3.27.3 and master 3.29.0. A separate button (not in the suffix) also reproduces the issue, ruling out focus as the cause. CC'd to @cbracken; no fix yet.
summaries.96112: A PR #96276 was submitted to fix this. A comment notes this is a duplicate of #79168 and is blocked on #39420. Comments as of 3.32.2 confirm the issue persists. No confirmed landed fix; status is stalled and still open.
summaries.104604: Comments identify the root cause: when iOS has a creditCardNumber autofill hint combined with a non-default textInputAction (Flutter defaults to UIReturnKeyDone), iOS falls back to the alphanumeric keyboard. Workaround confirmed: setting textInputAction: TextInputAction.unspecified restores the numeric keyboard. An engine PR (flutter/engine#17493) was filed but the issue persists on 3.22.0; status is open with a known workaround.
summaries.155336: Confirmed reproducible on iOS 14 devices (not iOS 13 or older) running Safari; Android and older iOS not affected. The double-tap causes select-all to fire before the context menu can stabilize, overriding the paste menu. Issue is OS-version specific. A later comment reports related issues on Chrome/Android in newer Flutter versions. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.87257: The issue is device-specific: iPad Pro 12.9-inch 4th gen has a different software keyboard layout (with dedicated accent keys) compared to iPad Air, which produces the extra dead-key character before the accented letter. A commenter reports reproducing the Spanish version of the bug. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.162066: Comments confirm the issue reproduces with the outlined steps (Windows Hello protecting Chrome password manager). The hypothesis is that the field loses focus during the Windows Hello PIN prompt, and Flutter cannot autofill into a non-focused field. Linked to related issues #145738, #152618, #137760, #61383. A HtmlElementView-based workaround is discussed but described as very hacky and only partially working. Status: open, stalled.
summaries.68547: Comments confirm the issue affects Android (even without hardware keyboard, via Gboard's soft arrow keys), not just desktop. On Android the active subrange is shown with aqua-green highlight in native apps but Flutter shows no highlighting and the caret stays at the end of the composing region. An engine PR #49314 partially fixed macOS. Issue was reopened after that partial fix. Currently P1 with a note that it has had no monthly updates.
summaries.103094: Comments debate whether to use unbounded message buffers, send full state instead of deltas, or add a framework-initiated synchronization message. @stuartmorgan argues for keeping APIs in embedder.h for ABI stability (the engine is shipped as a DLL). The discussion is architectural; no concrete fix PR is linked and the status is stalled.
summaries.97775: Comments identify the root cause: the Android embedding restarts the IME whenever the composing region changes, which was a prior fix to a different bug. One commenter notes the framework needs a way to signal that a composing change originated from a key event so the IME restart can be skipped. A GIF from @Renzo-Olivares shows the basic text client working correctly, suggesting the issue may be specific to document-editor use cases. Status: stalled.
summaries.135406: Confirmed on latest stable and master. The issue is that arrow and tab keys used for Chinese IME candidate selection are consumed by Flutter's key handling and not passed to the IME candidate window, affecting use with physical keyboards (Magic Keyboard, Bluetooth). No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.79158: Confirmed on web/CanvasKit in multiple browser setups; right-clicking in the context of a SelectableText shows the browser's generic context menu instead of Flutter's copy/paste menu. The report links a similar issue #78877. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open.
summaries.157771: Comments narrow the scope: the issue is emulator-specific (confirmed on Pixel 4 API 34 emulator and Samsung Galaxy S10 tablet, but not on Pixel 7 or Pixel Tablet physical devices). The incorrect PhysicalKeyboardKey USB HID usage is logged but does not functionally break backspace or enter. Assigned to team-android after internal triage. Status: open, under investigation.
summaries.163475: Root cause identified by a commenter: the Shortcuts widget inside _RawMenuAnchorState unconditionally registers arrow-key shortcuts that consume the events before TextField can process them. A proposed fix is to only register those shortcuts when the menu is open. No PR yet; status is open.
summaries.87124: Confirmed on macOS desktop in addition to web: vertical mouse drag inside a multiline TextField scrolls instead of selecting text. A related issue #59141 is linked. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.143773: Comments explain the root cause: RenderEditable has its own independent selection implementation that does not participate in SelectionArea's selection. @Renzo-Olivares was working on unifying the two systems, which would fix this for free. Status: open, work-in-progress on unification.
summaries.99573: Comments identify the scenario: a page not at the forefront has an autofocus TextField, and when the underlying page rebuilds and re-adds a TextField with autofocus, it steals focus from the top page. Proposed workaround: wrap background pages with ExcludeFocus. Also reproduces inside a PageView when multiple pages have autofocus TextFields. @gspencergoog was CC'd. Status: open, stalled.
summaries.116658: Comments confirm the proposal: SingleActivator is not keyboard-layout-agnostic. @gspencergoog explains that CharacterActivator should be used instead (e.g., CharacterActivator('+', control: true)) for layout-agnostic shortcuts, as it activates on the produced character rather than the physical key combination. Qt and GTK+ handle this correctly. Issue is labeled as a proposal; no framework change planned.
summaries.165204: Comments confirm the issue reproduces only in golden tests, not at runtime: the Ahem test font (used by default in matchesGoldenFile) does not include the U+25CF bullet character, rendering it as a tofu box. A commenter suggests switching fonts or using loadAppFonts() properly. Status: open, P3.
summaries.159076: Confirmed on macOS after repeated tapping; does not reproduce on Android. A commenter links this to #98322 as a possible gesture-arena version of that issue. The reporter notes that moving the pointer before clicking makes the spurious onChanged call more likely. Workaround (wrapping TextField in GestureDetector with opaque hit test) appears to prevent it. Status: open.
summaries.167983: Discussion focuses on scope: @justinmc suggests a textEditingValue parameter (not just text) covering selection and composing, alongside an onChangedValue callback. A community contributor posts a draft implementation showing it working by recreating the internal controller on didUpdateWidget. No official PR or prioritization decision yet; status is under consideration.
summaries.159576: Confirmed on Samsung S21 Ultra with Samsung Keyboard only; Gboard does not reproduce. The keyboard action fails to update to 'Done' approximately 60% of the time when moving focus via the 'Next' action button. Reproduced on S10+ with latest stable and master. Status: open, Samsung-keyboard-specific, no fix.
summaries.79169: The only substantive comment is a CC to @Renzo-Olivares. No root cause analysis or fix is discussed in comments. Status: open, stalled.
summaries.149160: Confirmed on web and macOS but not Android, iOS, Windows, or Linux. Root cause hypothesis: the negative x-offset applied on iOS/macOS (iOSHorizontalOffset constant) may no longer match native cursor positioning. The issue may also be related to font metrics or DPR. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.170215: Root cause identified in comments: SelectableText uses EditableText internally and the cursor width (default 2px) contributes to the layout width, causing it to be wider than a plain Text widget. Setting cursorWidth: 0 reduces but does not eliminate the difference. A commenter suggests SelectionArea as a workaround; confirmed it works correctly. Status: open, P3.
summaries.155741: Root cause identified: Wayland's security model prevents apps from reading clipboard data when not focused, unlike X11. Confirmed on Fedora and KDE Neon (pure Wayland) but not on Ubuntu (which uses XWayland by default). A GTK app (Gedit) shows the same behavior, indicating this is a GTK/Wayland limitation rather than a Flutter-specific bug. The Flutter Linux team member acknowledged the issue but had no updates. Status: open, stalled.
summaries.92050: Comments identify that Sogou IME ignores composing-region position events during input but listens near commit events, causing the candidate window to appear at the previous field's position. A community fix is proposed: use selection.baseOffset instead of 0 when composingRange is invalid in setComposingRect, with an additional vertical offset. Another commenter notes Qt has the same issue with Sogou. Status: open, no landed fix.
summaries.171373: Comments indicate the inputAssistantItem (Apple Pencil shortcuts bar) is being treated by Flutter as a software keyboard, causing the Scaffold to resize. A team member notes this should not affect layout in native apps. A native Swift workaround using method swizzling to clear leadingBarButtonGroups and trailingBarButtonGroups is provided. One engine reference to inputAssistantItem is found. Labeled as a proposal requiring native implementation. Status: open.
summaries.133743: Confirmed iPad-specific: does not reproduce on iPhone or Android. The issue involves scrolling with one finger outside a multi-line TextFormField while keeping a second finger on the field, causing a gesture-arena lockup. No fix or PR; status is open and was previously assigned to @Renzo-Olivares then unassigned due to inactivity.
summaries.137619: Comments confirm the issue reproduces on the latest stable and master. A commenter links to a similar issue #126585. The reporter tried the flutter_screenutil workaround from that issue without success. The root cause relates to using MediaQuery.of(context).size.height inside InteractiveViewer with constrained: false, which does not account for keyboard insets. Status: open, P3.
summaries.107327: Comments confirm the issue is only on web and desktop (macOS, Windows, Linux); Android and iOS are not affected. The root cause is that SelectionArea requests focus on tap-down, briefly stealing focus from the child TextField before restoring it on tap-up. @chunhtai suggests requesting focus only after a drag starts. No fix or PR merged; status is open.
summaries.91064: Linux, Windows, and macOS embedding fixes were merged via engine PRs #28924, #28877, #28970. iOS and Android remain unresolved. The issue is marked with an umbrella symbol and the progress checklist shows iOS and Android as incomplete. Status: partially fixed, iOS/Android remain open.
